channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: My 3 AI Agents Have Been Running My Business Solo For a Month
item.description: SoftBank just dropped $40 billion into OpenAI. Not for chatbots. For agents. I've had three of them running my business for the past month. Here's what they're actually doing, what broke, and...

item.title: SOC 2 Cost Us $47K. Here
item.description: When we decided to get SOC 2 certified, i googled "how much does SOC 2 cost" and every result said "$20,000 to $100,000 depending on your organization." Thanks. Very helpful. So here's the actual...

item.title: Codacy vs CodeFactor: Code Quality Tools Compared (2026)
item.description: Quick Verdict Codacy and CodeFactor are both cloud-based code quality tools that connect to your Git repositories, analyze pull requests, and give your codebase a grade. On the surface, they look...

item.title: We Deserve Better Than JSON as a DSL
item.description: It always starts innocently, you're creating a new config or endpoint with some dynamic behavior, thinking "KISS - Keep It Stupidly Simple". What's simpler than JSON or TOML? Some key-value pairs,...

item.title: Rotifer v0.8: Iron Shell — Hardening Before Scaling
item.description: v0.8 is the release where we stopped adding features and started making everything bulletproof. Before expanding the protocol's attack surface, we needed to prove the foundation is solid. Why...

item.title: Master the Linux Terminal for Modern Data Analytics
item.description: Introduction In the high-stakes world of Data Analytics, your tools should never be your bottleneck. Most analysts can build a dashboard, but the elite 1% know how to handle data where it actually...

item.title: What makes a good hero section in the age of AI
item.description: The secret to a great hero section in 2026 isn't knowing how to code animations from scratch. It's knowing what to ask for. The new design literacy Two years ago, building a landing page like...

item.title: 🚀 Headlamp: A Modern Kubernetes UI You’ll Actually Enjoy Using
item.description: 🔹 1. Introduction Managing Kubernetes clusters via CLI (kubectl) is powerful—but let’s be honest, it can get overwhelming, especially when dealing with complex workloads, debugging issues, or...

item.title: ScaleOps raises $130M to improve computing efficiency amid AI demand
item.description: ScaleOps just raised $130M to tackle GPU shortages and soaring AI cloud costs by automating infrastructure in real time.

item.title: THE GLOBE
item.description: I first saw this idea in a post by Samuel Abada on Twitter, and it immediately made me want to build my own version in Flutter. cobe_flutter is my recreation of that experience, and the name...

item.title: Xbox’s next big games showcase happens on June 7th
item.description: It may be the end of March, but gaming companies are already getting ready for the summer. First up is Xbox, which just announced that it will be holding its next major showcase event on June 7th....

item.title: For $200 more, you can get a MacBook Air
item.description: It was January 2008, and Steve Jobs had just pulled the MacBook Air out of a manila envelope onstage at Macworld. Within minutes, Windows PC executives everywhere lost their minds. They grabbed...

item.title: The mad dash to build the future of multimedia
item.description: It's 1989. To play a video, listen to a song, or show photos on a desktop computer requires bolting on expensive hardware, built by a different company, using different software. There are no...

item.title: Apple @ 50
item.description: Fifty years ago, on April 1st, 1976, Apple Computer Company was founded. Today it’s one of the most valuable companies in the world, celebrated for producing ubiquitous products like the iPad and...

item.title: AI chip startup Rebellions raises $400 million at $2.3B valuation in pre-IPO round
item.description: The startup, which is planning to go public later this year, designs chips specifically for AI inference, another challenger to Nvidia's dominance.

item.title: The origin story of Apple’s long-running relationship with FoxConn
item.description: In 2025, Patrick McGee published Apple in China: The Capture of the World's Greatest Company, a deeply-reported look at the tech giant's investment in - and growing reliance on - China. The...

item.title: Mistral AI raises $830M in debt to set up a data center near Paris
item.description: Mistral aims to start operating the data center by the second quarter of 2026.

item.title: Qodo raises $70M for code verification as AI coding scales
item.description: As AI floods software development with code, Qodo is betting the real challenge is making sure it actually works.

item.title: Vivo launches X300 Ultra flagship ‘designed for professional photography’
item.description: Vivo has launched the X300 Ultra in China today, the company's latest flagship that really puts the "camera" in cameraphone. The device is "designed for professional photography," according to...

item.title: The Download: brainless human clones and the first uterus kept alive outside a body
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Inside the stealthy startup that pitched brainless human...

item.title: Apple II Forever!
item.description: When you think of Apple, you probably think of the iPhone, or maybe the Mac, or perhaps you've got fond memories of the iPod. But Apple's 50-year run of creating tech products that people fall in...

item.title: KitchenAid redesigned its iconic mixer so you can set an exact speed
item.description: KitchenAid calls its new Artisan Plus Stand Mixer the biggest upgrade to the appliance since 1955. If you've ever been frustrated by the limited speed settings on the brand's iconic mixers, you...

item.title: These retractable studded tires might save our roads, ears, and lungs
item.description: If you want to feel truly invincible when driving in the snow, you need a set of studded snow tires. They're illegal in some places, typically restricted to the frigid months of the year in...

item.title: The State of Secrets Sprawl 2026: 9 Takeaways for CISOs
item.description: Secrets sprawl isn't slowing down: in 2025, it accelerated faster than most security teams anticipated. GitGuardian's State of Secrets Sprawl 2026 report analyzed billions of commits across public...

item.title: Apple’s Camera Indicator Lights
item.description: A thoughtful review of Apple’s system to alert users that the camera is on. It’s really well-designed, and important in a world where malware could surreptitiously start recording. The reason it’s...

item.title: Starcloud raises $170 million Series A to build data centers in space
item.description: Starcloud becomes the fastest Y Combinator startup to reach unicorn status, just 17 months after demo day.

item.title: Steve Jobs and the greatest run of products in tech history
item.description: "I'm pleased to report to you that Apple's back on track." It was May of 1998, and Steve Jobs was about 10 months into his second stint leading the company he'd cofounded more than two decades...

item.title: Russian CTRL Toolkit Delivered via Malicious LNK Files Hijacks RDP via FRP Tunnels
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a remote access toolkit of Russian-origin that's distributed via malicious Windows shortcut (LNK) files that are disguised as private key folders. The...

item.title: The IRS Wants Smarter Audits. Palantir Could Help Decide Who Gets Flagged
item.description: Documents show the tax agency is testing a Palantir tool to surface “highest-value” audit and investigation targets from a maze of legacy systems.
